steps.title: Use the missing life stage
steps.content: Scientists observed adults, hatchlings, and eggs at the Octopus Garden, but no juveniles. Since the site is used exclusively for reproduction, the missing juvenile stage suggests that octopuses leave after hatching and return later as adults.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported claims
steps.content: B discusses population stability, which the text does not address. C introduces egg size, egg number, and nests, none of which are described. D says the site is a feeding ground, but the text says it is used for reproduction. The remaining statement explains why juveniles were absent. The answer is A.
